methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
FleetZero is a Series A transportation company founded by Steven Henderson and Mike Carter that has raised $43M to date. The valuation universe consists of eight private peers in the transportation sector, including companies such as REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public comparables exist in the narrowly defined peer set.

Of the two valuation methods assessed, only stage-progression analysis proved applicable. The peer-median method was excluded due to insufficient comparable data—while five same-sector peers exist, fewer than three companies met the tight filters of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier classification, rendering a peer-median approach unreliable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $43M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $86M for the transition from Series A to the next financing round.

The triangulated valuation range is $73M to $99M, with a median of $86M, derived from a single applicable method. This single-method reliance creates material uncertainty around the range. Key variables affecting valuation include the breadth and financial health of the comparable set, the appropriateness of a 2.0x step-up multiplier for FleetZero's specific stage dynamics, and market conditions affecting transportation technology funding. Additional operational metrics—unit economics, customer traction, or technology differentiation—would strengthen conviction in this range.
